goal: Make /app/events and /app/events/demo-event-1 read like one connected event preparation workspace that guides a returning user from event choice to in-room relationship action, while preserving all local, side-effect-free behavior.

success_criteria.description: GET /app/events first viewport leads with a current event preparation priority: which event matters now, who to meet there, why it matters, readiness status, and the next safe action before broad event inventory or diagnostics.
Visible event cards offer readable paths into the event detail view and show venue, attendee opportunity, readiness, and relationship value as user-facing event work rather than technical inventory.

GET /app/events/demo-event-1 first viewport carries the selected event forward with source story, venue/time confidence, first person to meet, opening line context, and a local want-to-connect action; raw evidence IDs are not dominant primary copy.

The event decision preview and event-detail intent preview explicitly show no calendar update, saved-record write, realtime presence, peer notification, external message, notification, or outside network request occurs; reloading the same action route remains stable.

List and detail empty, pending, and failure states keep meaningful recovery controls; page tests assert the event-to-detail narrative, visible detail links, action safety ledger, route recovery, and copy guardrails; npm test and npm run build pass with no feature, service, API, fixture, shared UI, shell, contact, follow-up, chat, dashboard, agent, profile, or home route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing feature contracts, services, service factories, API envelopes, route handlers, or sample data fixtures
Changing global app shell, global CSS, shared UI primitives, contacts, contact acquisition, profile, follow-ups, chat, dashboard, agent, or home routes
Adding live calendar, CRM, notification, messaging, storage, search, AI, realtime presence, or network integrations
Changing post-event review, attendee import, recommendation, or want-to-connect feature behavior outside the route composition layer
